title: Tool: move_money — reallocate between two categories
description:
Implements YNAB Rule 3 ('roll with the punches').

Convenience tool: takes amount, from_category_id, to_category_id, month, budget_id. Internally:
1. GETs current budgeted for both categories that month
2. PATCHes from_category to (current - amount)
3. PATCHes to_category to (current + amount)

Annotation: MUTATING (not idempotent — running twice doubles the move).

Caveat: not transactional in YNAB. If step 3 fails, document that the user must manually reconcile or rerun. Consider an explicit error message that includes the partially-applied state.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add). 
- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.
- After mcp-ynab-0bw lands, this task targets a distinct module — fully parallel.
- Pre-split: still safe to fan out; coordinator does trivial 3-way merge on server.py tail.
- Can run alongside ANY other 'parallel-safe' task in the same wave label (wave-1, wave-2, wave-3).
- Cannot start until its 'depends-on' dependencies (if any) are closed; check bd dep tree.

title: Tool: assign_money — set budgeted for a category in a month
description:
Implements YNAB Rule 1 ('give every dollar a job').

Add @mcp.tool wrapping PATCH /budgets/{id}/months/{month}/categories/{cat_id} with body { category: { budgeted: <milliunits> } }.

Args: budget_id, category_id, amount (dollars, will be ×1000), month (str|'current'). Annotation: IDEMPOTENT_MUTATING_TOOL.

Note: this *sets* (not deltas) the budgeted amount. A separate move_money tool handles delta workflows.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add). 
- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.
- After mcp-ynab-0bw lands, this task targets a distinct module — fully parallel.
- Pre-split: still safe to fan out; coordinator does trivial 3-way merge on server.py tail.
- Can run alongside ANY other 'parallel-safe' task in the same wave label (wave-1, wave-2, wave-3).
- Cannot start until its 'depends-on' dependencies (if any) are closed; check bd dep tree.

title: Tool: get_month — monthly snapshot with RTA & Age of Money
description:
Add @mcp.tool that calls GET /budgets/{id}/months/{month}.

Args: budget_id (str), month (str, ISO date for first-of-month, or 'current' alias). Returns markdown:
- Month header
- Ready-to-Assign (to_be_budgeted/1000)
- Age of Money
- Income / Budgeted / Activity totals
- Per-category-group table: name, budgeted, activity, balance, goal status

This is the budget dashboard. The single highest-leverage missing read.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add). 
- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.
- After mcp-ynab-0bw lands, this task targets a distinct module — fully parallel.
- Pre-split: still safe to fan out; coordinator does trivial 3-way merge on server.py tail.
- Can run alongside ANY other 'parallel-safe' task in the same wave label (wave-1, wave-2, wave-3).
- Cannot start until its 'depends-on' dependencies (if any) are closed; check bd dep tree.

title: Budgeting Core: assign/move money + monthly view
description:
The server is a transaction logger; it cannot do the YNAB Method's core verbs.

Missing capabilities:
- Rule 1 (give every dollar a job): assign $ to a category for a month
- Rule 3 (roll with the punches): move $ from category A → B
- Rule 4 (age your money): read Age of Money / Ready to Assign
- Monthly snapshot: budgeted vs activity vs balance per category

This epic covers the highest-leverage gap: making the server usable for the actual work people open YNAB to do. Children include get_month/get_category_for_month tools, assign_money/move_money mutations, and month-snapshot MCP resources.

YNAB API endpoints involved:
- GET /budgets/{id}/months
- GET /budgets/{id}/months/{month}
- GET /budgets/{id}/months/{month}/categories/{cat_id}
- PATCH /budgets/{id}/months/{month}/categories/{cat_id}
notes:
PARALLEL DISPATCH PLAYBOOK
=========================
This corpus is labeled for an orchestrator LLM to fan out parallel sub-agents.

Labels:
  parallel-safe       — pure-add (new function only). Fan out freely.
  parallel-serialize  — modifies an existing function. Check zone:* label.
  parallel-gating     — blocks deeper parallelism (file split, helper factor).
  zone:<symbol>       — names a function/class. Tasks sharing a zone MUST serialize.
  wave-N              — recommended dispatch wave.

Run order:
  1. Wave 0  (gating):       mcp-ynab-0bw (server.py split) + g9z.7 (api helper)
                              → Land first to unlock true cross-file parallelism.
  2. Wave 1  (~17 agents):   all parallel-safe tools/resources from epics jwa/htf/3l3/g9z.
  3. Wave 1b (~5 agents):    parallel-serialize bug/typing fixes (each in its own zone).
  4. Wave 2  (~4 agents):    resources & elicitations whose dependencies cleared.
  5. Wave 2-serial-create_txn: qlh.1 → qlh.2 → qlh.3 (chain modifies create_transaction).
  6. Wave 3  (~6 agents):    @mcp.prompt definitions, all parallel-safe.

Conflict avoidance: the orchestrator should query 'bd show <id>' for each candidate,
read the zone:* labels, and refuse to dispatch two agents whose zone labels intersect.

Per-task hint: tasks include their own 'PARALLEL' note describing what they can /
cannot run alongside. The orchestrator should read those notes before fanning out.

title: Split server.py 735-line monolith into focused modules
description: server.py currently holds the FastMCP instance, all 9 tools, 2 resources, AsyncYNABClient, _get_client factory, YNABResources persistence class, markdown formatters, and helpers. Split into: client.py (AsyncYNABClient + _get_client + YNABResources), formatters.py (markdown table builders), tools.py (tool functions), resources.py (resource handlers). Keep mcp = FastMCP('YNAB') in server.py as the import surface so existing entry point still works. This is a pure refactor — no behavior change. Land as its own commit so the diff is reviewable.
acceptance_criteria: server.py reduced to FastMCP instance + imports. New modules have focused responsibilities. All existing tests pass with no changes. mcp-ynab CLI still launches.
notes:
Deferred from session 2026-05-03. The split is feasible but riskier than its value because tests/test_tools.py patches server.BudgetsApi/AccountsApi/etc and references server.get_budgets, server.ynab_resources, server.YNABResources directly. Safe approach for next attempt: keep mcp = FastMCP, ynab_resources, and YNABResources in server.py; move helpers and tool functions into formatters.py / tools.py / client.py; import the new modules at the BOTTOM of server.py so @mcp.tool registration runs after mcp is bound, AND re-export the moved tool callables so server.get_budgets etc still resolve. Land separately.
PARALLELISM MULTIPLIER — promoted to P1.

This is no longer a code-cleanliness task. With server.py split into focused modules (mcp_ynab/tools/budgeting.py, transactions.py, payees.py, analysis.py; mcp_ynab/resources.py; mcp_ynab/prompts.py; mcp_ynab/elicit.py), the ~17 pure-add tool/resource tasks in the new corpus become FULLY parallelizable across distinct files instead of merge-conflict-serializable on a single file.

Recommended split target:
  src/mcp_ynab/
    server.py            # FastMCP instance, AsyncYNABClient, registration glue (re-export tools/resources/prompts for test patch compatibility)
    api_helpers.py       # _get_client, _resolve_config_dir, APIs(client) factory (g9z.7)
    formatters.py        # _build_markdown_table, _format_accounts_output, _format_dollar_amount
    state.py             # YNABResources
    tools/
      budgeting.py       # get_month, assign_money, move_money, get_category_for_month
      transactions.py    # create/categorize/approve/update/delete/bulk/split/import
      analysis.py        # spending_by_*, get_transactions_by_category
      payees.py          # get_payees, rename_payee, scheduled
      diagnostics.py     # ping/whoami
    resources.py         # all @mcp.resource decorators
    prompts.py           # all @mcp.prompt decorators
    elicit.py            # shared elicitation helpers (_resolve_budget_id, etc.)

Re-export pattern for backward-compat with existing tests that patch server.BudgetsApi etc.: import * from submodules at the bottom of server.py.

DEPENDENTS: most P1/P2 tasks in epics jwa, htf, 3l3 become more dispatchable after this. Consider running this in a dedicated worktree first, then fanning out parallel agents on the new modules.
close_reason: Split server.py 763→99 lines into focused modules (client.py, formatters.py, state.py, resources.py, tools/budgeting.py, tools/transactions.py). All 42 unit tests pass; 9 integration tests collect cleanly; CLI launches; 10 tools + 2 resources register; 100% docstring coverage maintained. Tool modules access patched names via server.X for monkeypatch compatibility — rule documented in bd memory.

title: Add unit tests for all read-only MCP tools with mocked YNAB API
description: Five read-only tools currently have zero unit tests: get_budgets, get_accounts, get_categories, get_transactions, get_transactions_needing_attention. Resource handlers also lack content-shape tests. Use unittest.mock.AsyncMock to stub YNAB API classes (BudgetsApi, AccountsApi, etc.) inside _get_client. Replace inline DummyTransaction/DummyApi classes with shared conftest.py fixtures. Cover happy-path output, empty-list edge case, and one error path (ApiException) per tool.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 read-only tools have unit tests covering happy path, empty result, and error path. Resource handlers tested for content shape. Existing inline dummies replaced with conftest fixtures. task test passes.
notes: Done: 27 new tests in tests/test_tools.py covering all 5 read-only tools (get_budgets, get_accounts, get_transactions, get_transactions_needing_attention, get_categories) plus get_account_balance, set_preferred_budget_id, cache_categories, create_transaction, and the 2 resource handlers. Each tool covers happy path, empty-list, and (where applicable) error path. Coverage on server.py rose to 92%. Shared mock_ynab_apis fixture added to conftest.py. Migrating existing inline-Dummy categorize_transaction tests to the new fixture is deferred to follow-up.
close_reason: 27 unit tests added, 92% coverage on server.py, shared fixture established. Follow-up filed for dummy-class refactor in existing tests.

title: Feature: fuzzy + cached _find_category_id
description:
Today _find_category_id (server.py ~290) is exact case-insensitive against a fresh CategoriesApi.get_categories call. Two problems:
1. 'groceries' silently misses 'Groceries 🛒' or 'Groceries (& Household)'
2. Refetches every call instead of using YNABResources cache

Fix:
- Read from cache first; refresh on miss
- Use difflib.get_close_matches (cutoff=0.6) for fuzzy
- Return either a single id or a list of candidates so the caller can elicit choice (Epic 4)
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:_find_category_id + zone:YNABResources.
- Refactors _find_category_id and adds cache-read in YNABResources.
- Serialize after g9z.2 (also zone:YNABResources).
- BLOCKS qlh.2 (elicit category) — qlh.2 wants the multi-candidate return shape from this work.

title: Bug: get_account_balance ignores preferred_budget_id
description:
server.py ~339: get_account_balance always picks budgets[0] instead of consulting YNABResources.get_preferred_budget_id(). Inconsistent with create_transaction (~310), which does check.

Fix: use the same fallback chain as create_transaction. Even better — once Epic 4 elicit-budget lands, route through the shared _resolve_budget_id helper.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:get_account_balance + zone:create_transaction (uses preferred-budget helper).
- Modifies get_account_balance.
- Cannot run alongside qlh.1 (also touches the budget-resolution helper). Land g9z.1 first OR fold it into qlh.1.
- Safe alongside any task NOT labeled zone:get_account_balance or zone:create_transaction.

title: Elicit: category in create_transaction when missing/ambiguous
description:
Currently if category_name is unset or doesn't match exactly, the transaction posts uncategorized — silently.

Use ctx.elicit() to:
- Prompt for a category from the cached list when category_name is None
- When fuzzy match returns >1 candidate (Epic 6), present them and let the user pick

Skip elicitation if the model passed an explicit category_id (advanced path).

Depends on Epic 6 fuzzy matcher.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:create_transaction.
- Adds elicit-category logic to create_transaction.
- Depends on g9z.4 (fuzzy matcher returns candidate list).
- SERIAL CHAIN: must run AFTER qlh.1 (also touches create_transaction).
- Safe alongside other zones.

title: Elicit: budget when no preference set
description:
Today create_transaction & get_account_balance silently grab budgets[0] when YNABResources.preferred_budget_id is None. Silent foot-gun for users with multiple budgets.

Replace the fallback with ctx.elicit(...) presenting the budget list. On selection, optionally also call set_preferred_budget_id.

Touches: create_transaction (server.py ~310), get_account_balance (~339), and any future tool that defaults to budgets[0]. Factor a single helper: async def _resolve_budget_id(ctx) -> str.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:create_transaction + zone:get_account_balance.
- Replaces silent budgets[0] fallback in create_transaction AND get_account_balance with elicit-then-resolve helper.
- SERIAL CHAIN with qlh.2, qlh.3 on create_transaction. Run order: qlh.1 → qlh.2 → qlh.3.
- Conflicts with g9z.1 (overlaps on get_account_balance). Recommend: fold g9z.1 fix into this task, then drop g9z.1.
- Safe alongside ANY task that doesn't touch create_transaction or get_account_balance.

title: Prompt: monthly_budget_check
description:
@mcp.prompt for end-of-month / mid-month sanity check:
1. get_month for current month
2. Identify categories where activity > budgeted (overspending)
3. Suggest move_money from underspent categories to cover
4. Surface Age of Money trend if available

Args: budget_id, month (default 'current'). Output: prompt text guiding the model through the check.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add). 
- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.
- After mcp-ynab-0bw lands, this task targets a distinct module — fully parallel.
- Pre-split: still safe to fan out; coordinator does trivial 3-way merge on server.py tail.
- Can run alongside ANY other 'parallel-safe' task in the same wave label (wave-1, wave-2, wave-3).
- Cannot start until its 'depends-on' dependencies (if any) are closed; check bd dep tree.

title: Prompt: weekly_review
description:
@mcp.prompt walking the assistant through the weekly cleanup workflow:
1. Call get_transactions_needing_attention (uncategorized + unapproved, last 7 days)
2. For each batch of similar payees, propose a category from cache
3. Confirm with the user (elicitation if available, else inline)
4. Call bulk_categorize then approve_transactions

Args: budget_id (optional, falls back to preferred), days_back: int = 7.

Returns prompt text — no API calls in the prompt itself.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add). 
- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.
- After mcp-ynab-0bw lands, this task targets a distinct module — fully parallel.

title: Tool: bulk_categorize
description:
Categorize many transactions in one call. Args: budget_id, assignments (list[dict] with transaction_id + category_id).

Internally uses PATCH /transactions (the bulk endpoint) — one round-trip vs. N. Returns a markdown table: id → result.

Crucial for the 'categorize 17 Amazon charges' workflow that's painful one-at-a-time.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add). 
- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.
- After mcp-ynab-0bw lands, this task targets a distinct module — fully parallel.

- Can run alongside ANY other 'parallel-safe' task in the same wave label (wave-1, wave-2, wave-3).

title: Tool: update_transaction (PATCH any field)
description:
Generic field updater for a single transaction. Args: budget_id, transaction_id, plus optional memo, payee_name, amount (dollars), txn_date (ISO), flag_color, cleared, approved, category_id.

Internally PATCH /transactions/{id} with only the supplied fields. Returns the updated transaction as a dict.

Replaces several future single-purpose tools (rename_payee_on_txn, change_memo, ...). Annotation: MUTATING.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add). 
- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.

title: Tool: approve_transactions (bulk)
description:
Add @mcp.tool that approves N transactions without changing their categories.

Args: budget_id, transaction_ids (list[str]). For each, PATCH /transactions/{id} with { transaction: { approved: true } }. Returns markdown table: id → status.

Annotation: IDEMPOTENT_MUTATING_TOOL (re-approve is no-op).

Enables 'approve everything I just categorized' as a single round-trip.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add). 
- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.

title: Bug: categorize_transaction does full PUT instead of PATCH
description:
Today categorize_transaction (server.py ~430-480) re-PUTs the whole ExistingTransaction (account_id, date, amount, payee, memo, cleared, approved, flag, subtransactions...) just to set category_id.

Risks:
- Clobber on concurrent edit: if user updates memo in the YNAB app between our GET and PUT, we lose the new memo.
- Subtransactions get re-shipped — risk of breaking splits.
- Heavier payload.

Fix: switch to PATCH equivalent — pass only category_id in ExistingTransaction. Confirm with the SDK whether ExistingTransaction supports partial fields, or build the dict directly.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:categorize_transaction.
- Modifies the existing categorize_transaction function (PUT → PATCH).
- No other task currently in zone:categorize_transaction. Safe to fan out alongside any task in different zones.
- Conflicts: any future task that edits categorize_transaction.

title: Resource: ynab://months/{budget_id}/current
description:
@mcp.resource exposing this month's snapshot (budgeted/activity/balance per category, RTA, AoM) without a tool call.

Reuses the get_month tool's underlying logic; output formatted as TextContent. The 'current' literal means first-day-of-current-month server-side.
notes:

title: Tool: get_category_for_month
description:
Add @mcp.tool wrapping GET /budgets/{id}/months/{month}/categories/{cat_id}.

Args: budget_id, category_id, month (str|'current'). Returns: budgeted, activity, balance, goal_type, goal_target, goal_percentage_complete, note.

Useful for 'how much do I have left in Groceries this month?' without dragging the full month.
notes:

title: Elicitations for safer interactive tool flows
description:
The server has silent-failure modes that erode trust:
- create_transaction & get_account_balance silently grab budgets[0] when no preferred budget is set
- create_transaction silently accepts category_name='' or a misspelled name and posts uncategorized
- mutations never confirm before committing

MCP elicitation lets a tool pause mid-execution and ask the user for structured input. Use it to:
- elicit a budget when none preferred (replace silent budgets[0] fallback)
- elicit a category from the cached list when create_transaction's category_name is missing or fuzzy-matches multiple
- confirm before posting create_transaction
- confirm before delete_transaction (Epic 2)
- elicit source/destination categories in move_money (Epic 1)

mcp[cli]>=1.20 supports ctx.elicit() — no SDK upgrade needed.
notes:
PARALLEL DISPATCH PLAYBOOK
=========================
This corpus is labeled for an orchestrator LLM to fan out parallel sub-agents.

Labels:

  parallel-serialize  — modifies an existing function. Check zone:* label.
  parallel-gating     — blocks deeper parallelism (file split, helper factor).
  zone:<symbol>       — names a function/class. Tasks sharing a zone MUST serialize.
  wave-N              — recommended dispatch wave.

Run order:
  1. Wave 0  (gating):       mcp-ynab-0bw (server.py split) + g9z.7 (api helper)
                              → Land first to unlock true cross-file parallelism.
  2. Wave 1  (~17 agents):   all parallel-safe tools/resources from epics jwa/htf/3l3/g9z.
  3. Wave 1b (~5 agents):    parallel-serialize bug/typing fixes (each in its own zone).
  4. Wave 2  (~4 agents):    resources & elicitations whose dependencies cleared.
  5. Wave 2-serial-create_txn: qlh.1 → qlh.2 → qlh.3 (chain modifies create_transaction).
  6. Wave 3  (~6 agents):    @mcp.prompt definitions, all parallel-safe.

Conflict avoidance: the orchestrator should query 'bd show <id>' for each candidate,
read the zone:* labels, and refuse to dispatch two agents whose zone labels intersect.

Per-task hint: tasks include their own 'PARALLEL' note describing what they can /
cannot run alongside. The orchestrator should read those notes before fanning out.

title: MCP prompts for budgeting workflows
description:
Zero @mcp.prompt definitions exist today. Prompts are user-invocable templates (slash-commands in clients like Claude Desktop) that drastically reduce the prompt-engineering users have to do for repetitive workflows.

Add prompts for the workflows users actually run weekly/monthly:
- weekly_review (txns needing attention → propose categories → confirm)
- monthly_budget_check (budgeted vs activity, flag overspending, suggest reallocations)
- move_money (interactive A→B reallocation)
- fund_sinking_categories (Rule 2: true expenses)
- categorize_recent (one account or all, with auto_apply arg)
- spending_by_payee (top N, period-over-period)

These are mostly text — small lift, large UX gain. Most depend on tools landing first (move_money depends on Epic 1; categorize_recent benefits from bulk_categorize from Epic 2).

title: Transaction lifecycle: approve, edit, delete, split, bulk
description:
Today the only mutations are create_transaction and categorize_transaction (and the categorize path is heavyweight — it re-PUTs the entire transaction).

Round out CRUD so the assistant can run weekly/monthly review cleanly:
- independent bulk approve
- generic field update (memo, payee, date, amount, flag)
- delete
- bulk categorize
- splits / subtransactions
- trigger-import for connected accounts

Also fixes the existing categorize_transaction to use PATCH (just category_id) instead of full PUT, eliminating clobber-on-concurrent-edit risk.

YNAB endpoints: PATCH/PUT/DELETE /transactions/{id}, PATCH /transactions, POST /accounts/{id}/transactions/import.

title: Standardize all repo commands on uv (Taskfile, docs, CI)
description: User requested standardizing on uv for all work in this repo. Done in this commit: Taskfile.yml test/coverage/fmt commands now use 'uv run'. Added 'task lint' and 'task typecheck' commands. Remaining: ensure README and any future CI workflow use uv consistently. Document in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md that all Python commands should be invoked via uv run.
acceptance_criteria: Taskfile uses uv for all Python commands. README documents uv as the toolchain. Any new CI workflow uses uv. AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md mentions the convention.
close_reason: Section 6 housekeeping done: removed AGENTS.md duplicate Landing the Plane section, expanded README with overview/install/env/tools list/test instructions, added .github/workflows/ci.yml (uv setup, ruff, interrogate, pytest), and finalized uv standardization in Taskfile.

title: Add minimal GitHub Actions CI workflow (lint + unit tests)
description: No .github/workflows/ exists today. Add a minimal workflow that runs on push and PR: uv sync, ruff check, ruff format --check, pytest (unit tests only — integration excluded since CI has no YNAB key). Target Python 3.12. Keep it small — one job, one matrix entry initially.
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists. Runs lint + unit tests on push and PR. Green on main.
notes: Adding minimal CI workflow with uv setup, lint, docstrings, unit tests.
close_reason: Section 6 housekeeping done: removed AGENTS.md duplicate Landing the Plane section, expanded README with overview/install/env/tools list/test instructions, added .github/workflows/ci.yml (uv setup, ruff, interrogate, pytest), and finalized uv standardization in Taskfile.

title: Make get_transactions accept optional since_date parameter
description: get_transactions in server.py currently hardcodes since_date = datetime.now().replace(day=1).date(), so callers can only ever fetch the current calendar month. Accept an optional since_date parameter (ISO date string or date object). Default to start-of-month for backward compatibility. Add unit test for default behavior and explicit-date behavior. Add integration coverage too.
acceptance_criteria: get_transactions accepts optional since_date param. Default behavior unchanged. Unit + integration tests cover both paths.
notes: Adding since_date: Optional[date] = None to get_transactions. Default unchanged.
close_reason: Section 3 quirks done: get_transactions accepts optional since_date, AsyncYNABClient.__aexit__ explained as intentional no-op (verified ApiClient.__exit__ is also pass), pyproject.toml has authors/license/urls/classifiers/keywords and bumped to 0.2.0.

title: Build real-API integration test suite for mcp-ynab tools
description: Only one connection smoke test exists today. Create tests/integration/ structure with read-only tests against the user's real YNAB account: list budgets, fetch accounts, fetch transactions, fetch categories. Mutating tools (create_transaction, categorize_transaction) require explicit YNAB_INTEGRATION_ALLOW_WRITES=1 opt-in env var and target a designated sandbox. Document run instructions in README.md (YNAB_API_KEY=... task test:integration).
acceptance_criteria: tests/integration/ contains read-only integration tests for all read tools. Mutating tool tests gated behind YNAB_INTEGRATION_ALLOW_WRITES=1. README documents how to run. task test:integration passes with a valid YNAB_API_KEY.
notes: API key available. Building tests/integration/ with read-only tests for get_budgets/get_accounts/get_transactions/get_categories/get_transactions_needing_attention. Mutating-tool tests gated behind YNAB_INTEGRATION_ALLOW_WRITES=1 and skipped by default.
close_reason: 7 read-only integration tests pass against real YNAB (get_budgets, get_accounts, get_account_balance, get_transactions, get_transactions+since_date, get_transactions_needing_attention, get_categories). Mutating-tool tests gated behind YNAB_INTEGRATION_ALLOW_WRITES=1 with a documented placeholder; real write tests deferred until a sandbox budget is designated. Run with: uv run pytest -m integration

title: Raise docstring coverage to 80% in src/mcp_ynab/server.py
description: CodeRabbit pre-merge check on PR #3 flagged docstring coverage at 43.64% vs 80% threshold. Advisory warning, did not block merge. Add docstrings to public functions/classes in src/mcp_ynab/server.py to clear it.
notes: Running interrogate to baseline current coverage.
close_reason: Docstring coverage at 100% (was 81.8% baseline, originally 43.64% on PR #3). Added module + AsyncYNABClient + YNABResources docstrings. Wired interrogate into pyproject.toml [tool.interrogate] with fail-under=80 and added 'task docstrings' command.

title: Chore: document amount sign convention in create_transaction
description:
server.py: create_transaction takes amount: float with description 'Amount in dollars'. Doesn't say which sign means outflow.

Update the Field description: 'Amount in dollars. Negative for outflows (expenses, e.g. -42.50), positive for inflows (deposits, e.g. 1500.00).'

Also add a brief example in the function docstring.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:create_transaction (docstring only).
- Pure documentation change to create_transaction's amount Field description.
- Serial chain on create_transaction: g9z.5 → qlh.1 → qlh.2 → qlh.3, OR run g9z.5 last to avoid the noisy three-way merge.
- Cheapest in the chain — recommended FIRST so qlh.* sees the updated docstring.

title: Chore: Pydantic Literal for filter_type in get_transactions_needing_attention
description: Today filter_type is str + a hand-rolled '.lower() not in [...]' check that returns an error string (not raises). Replace with Literal['uncategorized','unapproved','both'] in the Field annotation; let MCP/Pydantic enforce upstream and remove the runtime check.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:get_transactions_needing_attention.
- Trivial: replaces a string check with Pydantic Literal in one function.
- No other task currently in this zone. Safe alongside any non-overlapping task.

title: Bug: cached category resource drops 'group' field
description:
YNABResources.cache_categories stores name + id + group (server.py ~280). But get_cached_categories returns only 'name (ID: id)' strings — the group is silently discarded.

Fix: include the group in the rendered TextContent ('Groceries [Immediate Obligations] (ID: ...)') and also expose a structured form for the model when possible.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:YNABResources.
- Modifies YNABResources.get_cached_categories (and the cached resource handler).
- Conflicts with g9z.4 (also zone:YNABResources). Serialize g9z.2 → g9z.4 (fuzzy/cache work).
- Safe alongside any task NOT labeled zone:YNABResources.

title: Tool: spending_by_category
description:
Aggregates transaction activity by category over a period. Args: budget_id, period: Literal['this_month','last_month','last_30d','last_90d','ytd'], top_n: int = 20.

Internally: GET /transactions with since_date, group by category_id, sum, sort. Returns markdown table: category, total spent, txn count, avg.

Distinct from category.activity in get_month — this can span months.

title: Elicit: source/destination categories in move_money
description:
When move_money is called without explicit IDs (or via the prompt), elicit:
- from_category: choose from categories with positive balance this month
- to_category: choose from any active category
- amount: default = abs(overspend in to_category) if applicable

Depends on Epic 1 (move_money) and Epic 6 (cached/enriched category resource).

description:
@mcp.prompt to walk the user through Rule 2 ('embrace your true expenses'). Help allocate to non-monthly categories: insurance, holidays, car maintenance, subscriptions.

Steps:
1. List categories with goals where this month's funded < target
2. Show how much is in Ready-to-Assign
3. Propose distribution
4. Apply via assign_money

Depends on Epic 1 (assign_money + get_month including goal_target).

@mcp.prompt for 'I overspent on X, take it from Y'. Drives the move_money tool with elicitation:
1. Elicit source category (filtered to those with positive balance)
2. Elicit destination category
3. Elicit amount (default = absolute overspend)
4. Confirm and call move_money

Depends on Epic 1 (move_money tool) and Epic 4 (category elicitation).

title: Correctness, hygiene, and resource expansion
description:
Bugs and small improvements surfaced in the design review, plus expansion of MCP resources for cheap context.

Bugs:
- get_account_balance ignores set_preferred_budget_id and always picks budgets[0]
- ynab://categories/{budget_id} resource drops the 'group' field that's already cached

Quality / typing:
- Pydantic Literal for filter_type in get_transactions_needing_attention (replace hand-rolled string check)
- fuzzy + cached _find_category_id (today: exact case-insensitive — 'groceries' silently misses 'Groceries 🛒')
- document amount sign convention in create_transaction (negative=outflow, positive=inflow)

New surface:
- ping / whoami tool (auth smoke test)
- ynab://budgets resource
- ynab://accounts/{budget_id} resource
- enriched ynab://categories/{budget_id} including current-month budgeted/activity

Refactor:
- factor a single helper for constructing the API stubs (BudgetsApi/AccountsApi/...) to reduce per-tool boilerplate.
notes:
PARALLEL DISPATCH PLAYBOOK
=========================
This corpus is labeled for an orchestrator LLM to fan out parallel sub-agents.

Labels:
  parallel-safe       — pure-add (new function only). Fan out freely.
  parallel-serialize  — modifies an existing function. Check zone:* label.
  parallel-gating     — blocks deeper parallelism (file split, helper factor).
  zone:<symbol>       — names a function/class. Tasks sharing a zone MUST serialize.
  wave-N              — recommended dispatch wave.

Run order:
  1. Wave 0  (gating):       mcp-ynab-0bw (server.py split) + g9z.7 (api helper)
                              → Land first to unlock true cross-file parallelism.
  2. Wave 1  (~17 agents):   all parallel-safe tools/resources from epics jwa/htf/3l3/g9z.
  3. Wave 1b (~5 agents):    parallel-serialize bug/typing fixes (each in its own zone).
  4. Wave 2  (~4 agents):    resources & elicitations whose dependencies cleared.
  5. Wave 2-serial-create_txn: qlh.1 → qlh.2 → qlh.3 (chain modifies create_transaction).
  6. Wave 3  (~6 agents):    @mcp.prompt definitions, all parallel-safe.

Conflict avoidance: the orchestrator should query 'bd show <id>' for each candidate,
read the zone:* labels, and refuse to dispatch two agents whose zone labels intersect.

Per-task hint: tasks include their own 'PARALLEL' note describing what they can /
cannot run alongside. The orchestrator should read those notes before fanning out.

title: Discovery & analysis: payees, scheduled txns, spending
description:
Round out the read surface so the assistant can answer 'what's coming up?' and 'where did the money go?'

Currently filters are limited to per-account + per-date. Missing read capabilities:
- payees list & rename/merge (autocomplete corpus + cleanup)
- scheduled transactions (upcoming bills — vital for planning)
- spending grouped by category (with period arg)
- spending grouped by payee (with period arg)
- transactions filtered by category

Also add ynab://payees/{budget_id} resource so the model gets payee autocomplete without burning a tool call.

YNAB endpoints: GET /payees, PATCH /payees/{id}, GET /scheduled_transactions, GET /transactions?category_id=…
notes:
PARALLEL DISPATCH PLAYBOOK
=========================
This corpus is labeled for an orchestrator LLM to fan out parallel sub-agents.

Labels:
  parallel-safe       — pure-add (new function only). Fan out freely.
  parallel-serialize  — modifies an existing function. Check zone:* label.
  parallel-gating     — blocks deeper parallelism (file split, helper factor).
  zone:<symbol>       — names a function/class. Tasks sharing a zone MUST serialize.
  wave-N              — recommended dispatch wave.

Run order:
  1. Wave 0  (gating):       mcp-ynab-0bw (server.py split) + g9z.7 (api helper)
                              → Land first to unlock true cross-file parallelism.
  2. Wave 1  (~17 agents):   all parallel-safe tools/resources from epics jwa/htf/3l3/g9z.
  3. Wave 1b (~5 agents):    parallel-serialize bug/typing fixes (each in its own zone).
  4. Wave 2  (~4 agents):    resources & elicitations whose dependencies cleared.
  5. Wave 2-serial-create_txn: qlh.1 → qlh.2 → qlh.3 (chain modifies create_transaction).
  6. Wave 3  (~6 agents):    @mcp.prompt definitions, all parallel-safe.

Conflict avoidance: the orchestrator should query 'bd show <id>' for each candidate,
read the zone:* labels, and refuse to dispatch two agents whose zone labels intersect.

Per-task hint: tasks include their own 'PARALLEL' note describing what they can /
cannot run alongside. The orchestrator should read those notes before fanning out.

title: Audit AsyncYNABClient.__aexit__ no-op against ynab SDK
description: AsyncYNABClient.__aexit__ silently does nothing today because ApiClient lacks a close() method in the version we use. Verify against the current ynab SDK version: does ApiClient now expose close() or aclose()? If yes, call it. If no, leave a one-line comment explaining the intentional no-op so the next reader doesn't think it's a bug.
acceptance_criteria: Either __aexit__ properly calls SDK cleanup, or contains a single comment explaining why it's a no-op. Decision documented.
notes: Verified: ApiClient.__exit__ is itself a no-op pass. Updating comment to explain this isn't a leak.
close_reason: Section 3 quirks done: get_transactions accepts optional since_date, AsyncYNABClient.__aexit__ explained as intentional no-op (verified ApiClient.__exit__ is also pass), pyproject.toml has authors/license/urls/classifiers/keywords and bumped to 0.2.0.

description:
Today's categories resource only returns 'name (ID: id)'. Enrich (or add a sibling resource) including current-month budgeted/activity/balance per category.

Lets the model answer 'what's left in Groceries?' from a resource read, no tool call needed.

Depends on Epic 1 (get_month logic) for the enrichment data path.

title: Chore: factor api-construction helper
description:
Every tool currently does e.g. accounts_api = AccountsApi(client); transactions_api = TransactionsApi(client); ... boilerplate.

Factor a small helper or dataclass: APIs(client) returns an object with .budgets / .accounts / .categories / .transactions / .payees / .scheduled. Reduces line count as more tools land in Epics 1, 2, 5.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-gating.
- Refactor: introduces APIs(client) factory consumed by every tool.
- Run alongside or just after mcp-ynab-0bw (server.py split). Both are gating; pair them.
- Once landed, all wave-1 tool tasks should rebase onto the new helper to avoid boilerplate divergence.

title: Tool: get_transactions_by_category
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add). 
- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.
- After mcp-ynab-0bw lands, this task targets a distinct module — fully parallel.
- Pre-split: still safe to fan out; coordinator does trivial 3-way merge on server.py tail.
- Can run alongside ANY other 'parallel-safe' task in the same wave label (wave-1, wave-2, wave-3).
- Cannot start until its 'depends-on' dependencies (if any) are closed; check bd dep tree.

title: Tool: rename_payee / merge_payees
description:
Wrap PATCH /payees/{id} for renaming. For merging (combine 'AMZN MKT' + 'Amazon.com' → 'Amazon'), iterate transactions for the source payee and PATCH them to point at the destination payee_id, then optionally delete the source.

YNAB doesn't have a true merge endpoint — document this as a multi-step operation.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-safe (pure-add).

title: Tool: split_transaction (subtransactions)
description:
Convert a single transaction into a split with subtransactions. Args: budget_id, transaction_id, splits (list of {amount, category_id, payee_name?, memo?}).

Validates Σ(splits) == parent amount (in milliunits). Constructs ExistingTransaction with subtransactions and PATCHes.

Enables 'this Costco run was $120 groceries + $30 gas' decomposition.
notes:

title: Refactor existing categorize_transaction tests to use mock_ynab_apis fixture
description: The new conftest fixture mock_ynab_apis (added in mcp-ynab-irw) replaces the inline DummyApi/DummyCtx/DummyTransaction classes used in tests/test_server.py for the categorize_transaction tests. Migrate test_categorize_transaction_uses_direct_lookup_for_id, test_categorize_transaction_reraises_non_404_api_exception, and test_categorize_transaction_preserves_existing_fields to use the shared fixture. Pure cleanup — no behavior change.
acceptance_criteria: Three existing categorize_transaction tests use mock_ynab_apis fixture. Inline Dummy* classes removed. All tests still pass.

value: mcp_ynab refactor rule: tool/resource modules MUST access tests-patched names (BudgetsApi, AccountsApi, CategoriesApi, TransactionsApi, ExistingTransaction, PutTransactionWrapper, ynab_resources, get_ynab_client) via 'server.<NAME>' attribute lookup, never via direct 'from ynab... import' in the tool module. Direct imports create local bindings that monkeypatch.setattr(server, ...) cannot reach. The SDK class re-exports in server.py are load-bearing and must keep their '# noqa: F401' markers — ruff will otherwise prune them as unused, breaking patches. New tool modules should follow the 'from .. import server as _s' pattern in tools/budgeting.py and tools/transactions.py.
